raw_text:
```json
{
  "claims": [
    "Gov. Gavin Newsom signed a bill allowing the University of California, Hastings College of the Law to remove the name of its founder.",
    "The bill specifies restorative justice measures for the Yuki and Round Valley Indian tribes.",
    "AB 1936 strikes the name of Serranus C. Hastings from the school's name.",
    "The California Legislature named the school in state statute in 1878 after Serranus C. Hastings paid $100,000 to the State Treasury.",
    "The signing of AB 1936 is a step toward healing a painful chapter for the Yuki and Round Valley people.",
    "AB 1936 enacts several effective restorative justice policies for the Yuki and Round Valley Indian people.",
    "AB 1936 includes initiatives such as naming the law library with an indigenous name and acknowledging the founder's history at college events.",
    "The bill allows the history and suffering of the Yuki and Round Valley people to be acknowledged.",
    "AB 923 requires state agency leaders to undertake training in properly communicating with tribes on government-to-government issues.",
    "AB 1314 creates a 'Feather Alert' to quickly find Native Americans missing under suspicious circumstances.",
    "AB 1703 encourages schools to engage with local tribes to provide accurate instruction about their culture and history.",
    "AB 2022 requires the renaming of California geographic features using the word 'squaw' as part of the name by January 1, 2024."
  ]
}
```
